input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
# .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & headers |
| **.NET SDK** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI (`dotnet`) |
| **.NET MAUI workload** | `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI templates & build targets |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload, or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |
| **Android SDK** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload, or via Android Studio | Emulators, platform APIs |

## 2. Recommended Install Order

```bash
# Step 1 — Install Xcode from the App Store, then accept the license
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install

# Step 2 — Install the .NET SDK (grab the latest LTS or STS from dot.net)
# Use the macOS installer (.pkg) for your arch (Apple Silicon = Arm64)

# Step 3 — Install the MAUI workload (pulls Android SDK + JDK automatically)
sudo dotnet workload install maui

# Step 4 (optional) — Install Android Studio if you want the emulator GUI
# https://developer.android.com/studio
```

## 3. Discovering the Right Versions

```bash
# See which .NET SDKs are available upstream
dotnet sdk check

# See installed workloads and whether updates exist
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload update          # pulls latest compatible versions

# Check Xcode version (MAUI docs list the minimum per release)
xcodebuild -version

# Check Android SDK components the workload installed
dotnet android sdk list         # or use Android Studio SDK Manager
```

The official compatibility matrix lives at:
**https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui#supported-platforms**

## 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios        # adjust TFM to your SDK version

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SimUDID>
# (find UDID with: xcrun simctl list devices available)

# Run on Android Emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

If all four commands succeed, your environment is fully functional.

## 5. Editor / IDE Options

| Editor | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit** | Free, lightweight, officially supported |
| **JetBrains Rider** | Full-featured, paid, excellent MAUI support |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** | **Retired** — no longer available |

## Key Gotchas

- **Apple Silicon**: install the **Arm64** .NET SDK, not x64.
- **Xcode updates** can break iOS builds — re-run `dotnet workload update` after upgrading Xcode.
- The MAUI workload auto-provisions the Android SDK under `~/Library/Android/sdk`; set `ANDROID_HOME` if you need a custom location.
- For **physical iOS devices**, you need an Apple Developer account and provisioning profile.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking for a comprehensive setup guide for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. This is a knowledge/guidance question - they explicitly say they can't run commands right now, so they want a plan, not execution.

Let me provide a thorough, well-organized answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI setup on macOS.
